source: US Census Bureau, Population Estimates Program (PEP)
decision_reason: Selected: gives a credible market-size denominator; available per state per year 2011-2014; joins cleanly on State+Year from a key-free CSV.

source: US Census Bureau, Small Area Income and Poverty Estimates (SAIPE)
decision_reason: Selected: proxies household purchasing power; published for every state per year 2011-2014; key-free static files keep it fully reproducible.

source: US Bureau of Labor Statistics, Local Area Unemployment Statistics (LAUS)
decision_reason: Selected: captures local economic stress during the 2011-2014 recovery; monthly state series via key-free public API, aggregated to annual rate.

candidate_dataset: Median household income by state (ACS B19013)
source: US Census Bureau, American Community Survey API
decision_reason: Rejected: the ACS data API now returns 'Missing Key' (verified 2026-05-30) and requires registered credentials; SAIPE supplies an equivalent state median-income series without a key, so SAIPE was chosen for reproducibility.

source: US Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA), SAGDP
decision_reason: Rejected: state GDP measures production, not consumer demand, and overlaps with population as a scale measure; it would duplicate the market-size angle already covered.

source: US Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA), SAINC1
decision_reason: Rejected: highly collinear with SAIPE median household income; keeping a single income measure avoids redundancy in an 11-page report.

source: US Bureau of Labor Statistics, CPI-U
decision_reason: Rejected: CPI is published only for four broad census regions and selected metros, not all states, so it cannot join cleanly on State+Year and would force a coarse region-level merge.

source: US Department of Labor, Wage and Hour Division
decision_reason: Rejected: low cross-state variation in this period and only a weak theoretical link to retail margin and discount behaviour.
